data.title: Matador Resources on the rise after Q3 beat, higher 2024 production plans
data.description:
Matador Resources (NYSE:MTDR) +0.7% in Wednesday's trading after posting Q3 adjusted earnings and revenues that topped analyst estimates, while raising guidance for FY 2024 oil production.

Q3 was "one of the best quarters in Matador's history, with record production and the closing of our largest acquisition to date," Chairman and CEO Joseph Foran said, referring to the recent acquisition of Ameredev assets, as output jumped 27% Y/Y to 171,480 boe/day, including ~100K bbl/day of oil.

The production gains were attributed to better than expected initial results from new wells drilled during Q3 in addition to continued strong performance of existing wells, especially the 21 gross Dagger Lake South wells that were part of the Advance acquisition in 2023.

On Matador's (MTDR) earnings conference call, executives pointed to the integration of new assets combined with improved drilling efficiencies and hydraulic fracturing multiple wells leading to the better than anticipated production results.

For the full year, Matador (MTDR) now expects total production of 167.5K-172.5K boe/day, up from its previous guidance of 158.5K-163.5K boe/day, including 98.5K-101.5K bbl/day of oil, compared to its prior view of 93.5K-96.5K bbl/day.

The company also forecast slightly higher than expected capital spending of $1.35B-$1.6B, citing accelerated completion of wells on newly acquired properties.

data.title: Him & Hers, WW down on Novo attempt to restrict compounded semaglutide
data.description:
• Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) and WW International (NASDAQ:WW) are both down significantly in Wednesday trading following Novo Nordisk's (NVO) attempt to get the U.S. FDA to ban the manufacture of compounded semaglutide.
  • The Danish drugmaker has asked the agency to have semaglutide placed on the Demonstrable Difficulties for Compounding List, arguing that compounded versions pose safety risks. Semaglutide is a blockbuster med for Novo and is the active ingredient in its weight loss and type 2 diabetes drugs, Wegovy and Ozempic.
  • Earlier this year, Him and Hers and WW International said they would offer compounded version of semaglutide at a fraction of the cost of the brand-name versions.
  • This was permissible as Novo has been experiencing a shortage of semaglutide for much of the year. However, that has now ended.
  • Novo rival Eli Lilly (LLY) recently sued three compounders for continuing to make and sell compounded tirzepatide, the active ingredient in its weight loss and diabetes meds Zepbound and Mounjaro.
  • Hims & Hers is down ~11%, while WW is down ~7%.

data.title: Grupo Rotoplas S.A.B. de C.V. reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Grupo Rotoplas S.A.B. de C.V. press release (OTCPK:GRPRF): Q3 EBITDA of Ps. 247 million
  • Net sales closed the quarter at Ps. 2,838 million, 6.0% below 3Q23

data.title: Iberdrola reports net profit of €5.47B for first nine months of the year
data.description:
• Iberdrola press release (OTCPK:IBDSF): Net profit of €5.47 billion and EBITDA of €13.27 billion for the first nine months of the year.
  • Performance in the United States (+25% EBITDA) and the United Kingdom (+15% EBITDA) are the main drivers of the results, the company said.

data.title: Akzo Nobel N.V. reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Akzo Nobel N.V. press release (OTCQX:AKZOF): Q3 revenue of €2.66B (-3% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted earnings per share from continuing operations is €1.14.
  • Based on current market conditions and constant currencies, AkzoNobel expects to deliver 2024 adjusted EBITDA of around €1.5 billion.

data.title: Element Solutions soars on report it's exploring sale
data.description:
Element Solutions (NYSE:ESI) jumped 11% after a report that the specialty chemicals maker is reviewing its options, including a sale.

The company has hired Bank of America and Goldman Sachs Group to gauge interest from possible acquirers, according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

The chemicals manufacturer, which is valued at $6.4 billion, is likely to garner interest from peers, as well as some private equity firms, according to the report. Deliberations are characterized as being at an early stage, and Element (ESI) may decide not to sell itself.

Representatives for Element Solutions, Bank of America and Goldman Sachs declined to comment to Bloomberg.

data.title: Apple iOS 18.1 rolls out next week, iOS 18.2 Beta now available
data.description:
Apple Intelligence (NASDAQ:AAPL) finally arrives to the iPhone as the iOS 18.1 update rolls out next week, while the iOS 18.2 Beta developer update is available now.

It remains to be seen if the arrival of Apple Intelligence features will lead to a spike in iPhone 16 demand, as several analysts have surmised.

The iOS 18.1 update will be released sometime early next week, likely on October 28. It will be compatible with all iPhone 16 models and the iPhone 15 Pro and Pro Max models. The Apple Intelligence features will initially only be available in U.S. English.

The update will include new writing tools, a new Siri interface, text and email summarizations, a "clean up" feature in Photos, improved audio transcriptions and Safari improvements.

iOS 18.2 introduces other English-speaking locations such as the U.K., Australia, Canada, New Zealand and South Africa.

The iOS 18.2 update also features artificial intelligence-powered image generation, an Image Wand that transforms rough images into detailed pictures. It also includes the anticipated Genmoji, which allows users to create their own emojis to share.

It builds on the writing tools introduced in the iOS 18.1 update, according to 9to5Mac. It also integrates OpenAI's ChatGPT into the writing tools feature and Siri.

Additional Apple Intelligence features are expected to arrive in early 2025.

Apple shares were down 2.7% by late market trading on Wednesday.

data.title: Altair Engineering jumps on report Siemens in talks to acquire
data.description:
Altair Engineering (NASDAQ:ALTR) rose 4% after a report that Siemens AG (OTCPK:SIEGY) is in talks about a possible takeover of the engineering software maker.

Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) is in working with advisers as it it discusses a potential deal for Altair (ALTR), according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Altair is exploring a sale.

The Bloomberg news comes after Reuters reported on Tuesday that the engineering software maker was exploring a sale after receiving takeover interest. Potential bidders include rival design software makers such as Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) according to the report. Reuters couldn't determine whether Cadence had approached Altair.

Earlier on Wednesday a Baird analyst wrote that a deal from Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) makes "less sense" after some recent acquisitions by Cadence.

Altair (ALTR) is scheduled to report Q3 results next Wednesday. Altair's short interest is 8.3%.
